eventMetadata.context_description: Partizan's slight edge as 52% implied probability favorite stems from their strong home-court advantage at Stark Arena and superior recent form, winning four of their last five EuroLeague games, while ASVEL grapples with a three-game road skid and the league's third-worst away scoring average. Competitive balance arises from ASVEL's potent backcourt duo of TJ Shorts and Chris Johnson, who could exploit Partizan's perimeter defense vulnerabilities exposed in recent losses, alongside head-to-head history showing tight contests—Partizan won their last meeting by just six. Key swing factors include official injury updates on Partizan's Mathias Lessort (questionable, ankle) or ASVEL's potential rest for star Youssoupha Fall, plus early-game momentum in this playoff-race clash.

description:

Primary driver remains complete absence of any authenticated posts from him, despite periodic unverified rumors linking anonymous accounts to his circle amid Iran's regional tensions, including recent Israel strikes and proxy escalations. No official statements confirm activity, prioritizing operational secrecy. Traders watch for surprises from domestic unrest or leadership signals before the market's resolution, but base rates of similar high-profile Iranian figures suggest sustained silence.

question: Will USD reach 1.6M Iranian rials by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the finalized USD exchange rate on Bonbast is equal to or above the specified price for any day between market creation and March 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve according to the daily finalized free-market USD exchange rate as displayed on Bonbast (https://www.bonbast.com/graph/usd), which publishes prices in Iranian toman, where 1 Iranian toman equals 10 Iranian rials (IRR).

A daily figure will be considered finalized once the following day’s figure is released.

Revisions or corrections to daily figures indicating a qualifying exchange rate will be considered only if they occur before all relevant figures for this market have been finalized.

The resolution source for this market will be Bonbast (https://www.bonbast.com/graph/usd). Resolution will occur once the March 31, 2026, exchange rate data is finalized. If the resolution source becomes permanently unavailable, another resolution source will be chosen.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects caution over further Iranian rial depreciation against the USD by March 31, 2025, driven primarily by the currency's recent plunge past 70,000 rials per dollar on free-market exchanges amid 37% annual inflation and constrained oil revenues from U.S. sanctions. President-elect Trump's pledge for "maximum pressure" policies has amplified devaluation fears, following the rial's 50% yearly loss tied to regional tensions including Israeli strikes on Iranian assets. Official central bank rates remain pegged near 42,000, but parallel markets dominate trader focus. Key upcoming catalysts include Trump's January inauguration, potential new sanctions designations, and Iran's March parliamentary elections, which could exacerbate economic pressures or prompt policy shifts.

question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

question: New "Stranger Things" episode released by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Netflix officially releases a new episode of Stranger Things (i.e., an episode that was not previously available to stream on Netflix) between market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, an “episode” must be listed as a distinct episode of Stranger Things on Netflix and be playable for general subscribers in the United States. A behind-the-scenes featurette, documentary, trailer, recap, cast interview, deleted scenes compilation, or other bonus content will not count unless it is clearly presented by Netflix as an official numbered or titled episode of the series.

If Netflix releases an alternate cut, extended cut, or “secret” version of an already-released episode, it will not count unless it is listed as a separate episode entry on Netflix.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the Stranger Things title page on Netflix (episode list and availability), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: ChatGPT Outage by March 27?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if OpenAI's ChatGPT experiences any incident classified as 'Partial/Full Outage' as of the time it is marked as “Resolved” by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Only incidents listing ChatGPT under 'Affected components' will be considered. Incidents labeled as affecting 'APIs,' or 'Sora,' but not ChatGPT, will have no bearing on the resolution of this market.

Classifications of an incident while it is ongoing will have no bearing on the resolution of this market. Only classifications of events that are resolved will be considered.

Qualifying incidents include outages and other issues classified as 'Partial/Full Outage' when they are resolved, during this market's above-specified timeframe. 

An incident resolved outside this market’s timeframe will only qualify if ongoing at this market’s resolution time, in which case the market will remain open until that incident is marked as “Resolved,” and resolution will be based on the first impact classification thereafter, regardless of subsequent revisions or corrections.

Revisions that upgrade an incident’s impact classification to 'Partial/Full Outage' will qualify if the incident was resolved and the revision is published within this market’s timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official system status information published by OpenAI on status.openai.com; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Critical Discord Incident by April 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Discord experiences any incident classified as Critical (red) by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Qualifying incidents may include outages and other issues that Discord classifies as Critical.

Revisions to the impact classification of any such incident will be considered as long as those revisions are published within this market’s timeframe. However, revisions of a previously published incident impact classification of Critical (red) to another classification will not disqualify an incident from counting.

If an incident is ongoing at this market’s resolution time, the market may remain open until that incident receives an official impact classification, and it will resolve based on the first such classification published, regardless of subsequent revisions or corrections.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Discord (for example, on discordstatus.com or discordstatus.com/history); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Note: Discord impact classifications of Major (orange) and Critical (red) are not equivalent.

This market will resolve to "Gen.G Esports" if Gen.G Esports win the match against Global Esports.

This market will resolve to "Global Esports" if Global Esports win the match against Gen.G Esports.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://vlr.gg. However, if https://vlr.gg has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gen.G Esports enters as heavy favorites in their VCT Pacific Group Alpha BO3 clash against Global Esports, with trader consensus implying over 80% win probability for the Korean squad amid their flawless 2-0 group stage run, including a gritty 2-1 upset over top seed Paper Rex. Global, winless at 0-2 after straight-set losses to DRX and Rex, shows roster inconsistencies despite flashy individual plays from SkRossi. Head-to-head favors Gen.G's prior 2-0 sweep, bolstered by duelist stars Lakia and t3xture thriving on Pacific map pools like Ascent and Lotus. No confirmed roster changes or bans noted, though Global's rest disadvantage after back-to-back defeats could amplify Gen.G's momentum in this do-or-die elimination matchup.

eventMetadata.context_description: Paper Rex holds a commanding edge in trader consensus for their VCT Pacific Group Alpha BO3 clash with Nongshim RedForce, reflecting their dominant 3-0 group stage run and head-to-head sweeps in recent encounters. PRX's Jinggg and d4v4i have shredded defenses on Bind and Ascent, boasting a 75% win rate post-patch 8.11, while RedForce limps in off back-to-back losses amid sloppy executes and a 1-5 map skid against top foes. No roster shifts or bans reported, but PRX's rest advantage after a lighter schedule bolsters their momentum; watch RedForce's underdog upset potential via Icebox vetoes, though historical data favors the Singaporean squad's playoff positioning.

question: Capitals vs. Blue Jackets
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 14 at 7:00PM ET:
If the Capitals win, the market will resolve to "Capitals".
If the Blue Jackets win, the market will resolve to "Blue Jackets".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Washington Capitals hold a clear edge in trader consensus for their matchup against the Columbus Blue Jackets, driven by their top spot in the Metropolitan Division and Alex Ovechkin's scorching pace toward Wayne Gretzky's goal record, with 853 tallies already. The Caps boast a 5-1-1 record in recent games versus Columbus, including a 4-3 overtime win on November 25, bolstered by strong home-ice advantage at Capital One Arena where they win 65% of contests. Blue Jackets struggle at 3-6-1 lately, hampered by injuries to Zach Werenski (shoulder) and Boone Jenner (out long-term), thinning their blue line and scoring depth. Watch for Caps' rest advantage after a lighter schedule, though Columbus' young guns like Kirill Marchenko could spark an upset if goaltending holds. Odds reflect 70% implied probability for Washington victory.

eventMetadata.context_description: Philadelphia Flyers lead trader consensus as favorites against the Montreal Canadiens, driven by home-ice advantage at Wells Fargo Center and Montreal's mounting injury concerns. Official reports confirm Habs center Kirby Dach (knee) and defenseman Kaiden Guhle (upper body) are sidelined, weakening their depth ahead of a potential back-to-back schedule stretch. Philly boasts a solid 3-2-1 start with balanced scoring from Travis Konecny and Matvei Michkov, while Canadiens limp in at 1-4-2, outscored in recent road games. Momentum favors the Flyers' power play efficiency, though Montreal's young core could spark an upset if goaltender Samuel Montembeault steals the show. Odds capture these dynamics amid unpredictable early-season volatility.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carolina's commanding 3-1 series lead over the New York Islanders, fueled by Frederik Andersen's playoff mastery (3-0 record, .930 save percentage including two shutouts), drives trader consensus toward the Hurricanes ahead of critical Game 5 at UBS Arena. The Hurricanes' offense has erupted for 16 goals in their last three wins, capitalizing on Ilya Sorokin's woes (0-3, .846 SV%), while Carolina's depth shines through regular-season dominance and home-ice edge in potential closeouts. Islanders' home desperation and crowd boost could spark an upset—playoffs reward resilience—but momentum favors Carolina unless lineup changes or Sorokin's rebound alters the script.

eventMetadata.context_description: Minnesota Wild's superior standing in the Central Division playoff race, bolstered by a 4-1-1 recent run and elite goaltending from Filip Gustavsson, anchors trader consensus favoring them heavily against the rebuilding Anaheim Ducks, who sit last in the Pacific with a 3-6-1 skid. Official injury reports confirm Ducks forward Trevor Zegras remains sidelined with an ankle issue, thinning their top-six scoring, while Wild captain Jared Spurgeon returns from suspension, strengthening their blue line. Home-ice edge at Xcel Energy Center and Wild's 6-2-2 home record amplify the edge, though Ducks' youth could spark an upset amid Wild's occasional lapses against weaker foes. Schedule density favors rested Wild post-bye.

eventMetadata.context_description: Winnipeg Jets face Utah Hockey Club in a pivotal Central Division NHL clash, where trader consensus leans toward Utah amid the Jets' 2-5-0 start plagued by injuries to Nikolaj Ehlers (knee, out indefinitely) and Kyle Connor's recent lower-body issue limiting practice time. Utah (5-2-1) rides momentum from a four-game win streak at home, bolstered by Clayton Keller's hot hand (10 points) and Vitek Vanecek's .915 save percentage in net. Jets rely on Connor Hellebuyck's Vezina form but face back-to-back fatigue after a loss to Dallas; Utah's rest edge and 3-1 home record vs. Jets historically tilt probabilities, though upsets loom in high-scoring affairs averaging 7.2 goals.

Jets rely on Connor Hellebuyck's Vezina form but face back-to-back fatigue after a loss to Dallas; Utah's rest edge and 3-1 home record vs. Jets historically tilt probabilities, though upsets loom in high-scoring affairs averaging 7.2 goals.

question: Will 'BULLY' - Ye debut week album sales be less than 300k?
description:
Kanye West's new album 'BULLY' is scheduled to be released on March 27, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the debut week sales for Kanye West's album 'BULLY', according to Hits Daily Double.

If the album 'BULLY' has not been released by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket.
 
If the album’s debut week sales fall exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher bracket.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the "HITS TOP 50" list found at https://hitsdailydouble.com/sales_plus_streaming, specifically, the figure in the column titled "Activity", once the results are finalized for the album’s debut week. If this resolution source becomes permanently unavailable, another credible resolution source may be chosen.

This market will resolve according to the final "Close" price of the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

question: T20I Series New Zealand vs South Africa, Women: New Zealand vs South Africa
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between New Zealand and South Africa scheduled for March 24 2026 in T20I Series New Zealand vs South Africa, Women.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: New Zealand Women enter the T20I series against South Africa Women as narrow favorites in trader consensus, buoyed by home advantage at Hagley Oval and a potent seam attack led by Lea Tahuhu and Jess Kerr, who thrive in seaming conditions. Recent squads confirm no major injuries, with Sophie Devine captaining a balanced NZ side fresh off domestic success, while South Africa relies on Laura Wolvaardt's batting and Nonkululeko Mlaba's spin post their T20 World Cup final run. Head-to-head records show NZ winning 7 of 12 T20Is, though SA's away form poses upset risk; schedule intensity peaks with back-to-back games in Christchurch, testing depth amid variable weather.
eventMetadata.league: T20I Series New Zealand vs South Africa, Women

question: T20I Series New Zealand vs South Africa, Women: New Zealand vs South Africa - Who wins the toss?
description:
This market refers to the pre-match coin toss for the cricket match between New Zealand and South Africa scheduled for 2026-03-24 in T20I Series New Zealand vs South Africa, Women.
This market resolves according to the official coin toss result (the team recorded as having won the toss) as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
If an official toss winner is recorded, the market will resolve based on that result even if the match is subsequently delayed, relocated, abandoned, or no ball is bowled.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market will remain open and resolve according to the toss result for the listed fixture when it is eventually played.
If the fixture is permanently canceled, forfeited before any toss is conducted, or otherwise completed without an official toss winner being recorded, the market will resolve 50-50.

description:

question: T20I Series New Zealand vs South Africa, Women: New Zealand vs South Africa - Toss Match Double New Zealand Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between New Zealand and South Africa scheduled for 2026-03-24 in T20I Series New Zealand vs South Africa, Women.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to New Zealand will be considered correct if  New Zealand is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to South Africa will be considered correct if South Africa is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

events.title: T20I Series New Zealand vs South Africa, Women: New Zealand vs South Africa - Toss Match Double

eventMetadata.context_description: New Zealand Women host South Africa Women in the opening T20I of their series, with trader consensus reflecting a razor-thin 51.5% implied probability across White Ferns victory, Proteas win, and draw amid high uncertainty. Both sides enter evenly matched after strong recent form—New Zealand sweeping India 3-0 at home last month, South Africa clinching a T20I series against England—fueling the deadlock. Home advantage and spin-friendly pitches tilt slightly toward the hosts' Amelia Kerr and Lea Tahuhu, but South Africa's pace attack led by Marizanne Kapp and Tazmin Brits' batting exploits even the scales. Toss looms large for dew-affected chases, while light rain risks bolster draw pricing, underscoring the competitive balance in this high-stakes opener.

question: T20I Series New Zealand vs South Africa, Women: New Zealand vs South Africa - Toss Match Double Draw
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between New Zealand and South Africa scheduled for 2026-03-24 in T20I Series New Zealand vs South Africa, Women.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to New Zealand will be considered correct if  New Zealand is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to South Africa will be considered correct if South Africa is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.

If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.

question: T20I Series New Zealand vs South Africa, Women: New Zealand vs South Africa - Most Sixes New Zealand Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between New Zealand and South Africa scheduled for 2026-03-24 in T20I Series New Zealand vs South Africa, Women.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to New Zealand will be considered correct if New Zealand is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than South Africa.The outcome corresponding to South Africa will be considered correct if South Africa is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than New Zealand.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

eventMetadata.context_description: New Zealand Women and South Africa Women enter their T20I showdown with closely matched six-hitting firepower, fueling trader consensus at 51.5% across NZL, ZAF, and draw outcomes for most sixes. Sophie Devine leads NZ's charge with 11 sixes across her last five T20Is, closely trailed by South Africa's Tazmin Brits (9) and Marizanne Kapp (8), per official ICC stats. No confirmed injuries disrupt either squad's powerplay or death-over aggressors, while the sedate Basin Reserve pitch historically yields 7-9 sixes per innings equally for visiting sides. Recent bilateral form shows parity—NZ averaging 8.1 sixes per match, SA 8.3—keeping the race razor-tight amid upset-prone T20 dynamics.

description:

question: Will the price of Solana be between $70 and $80 on March 25?
description:
This market will resolve according to the final "Close" price of the Binance 1 minute candle for SOL/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the SOL/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/SOL_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance SOL/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.
